questions.text: In which addition reaction Markownikov's rule is not obeyed:

questions.explanation: Markownikove's rule is obeyed by unsymmetrical alkenes Symmetrical alkanes like CH3CH-CHCH3 having no binding to obey the rule because both doubly bonded carbon atoms have same number of hydrogen atom.

questions.explanation: The alkene which contains only one double bond is called monoene.

questions.text: Baeyer's reagent is used in the laboratory for
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Alkaline KMnO4 is called Baeyer's reagent which is used in the laboratory for the detection of double bond.

questions.text: The only alkyne which gives an aldehyde upon the addition of water
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ethyne, also known as acetylene, is an alkyne with the chemical formula C2H2. When ethyne reacts with water in the presence of a mercuric sulfate catalyst, it undergoes an addition reaction to form acetaldehyde. Acetaldehyde is an aldehyde. Therefore, ethyne (acetylene) does give an aldehyde upon the addition of water.

Propyne is an alkyne compound with the chemical formula C3H4. When propyne reacts with water, it undergoes an addition reaction and forms a ketone, not an aldehyde.Therefore, propyne does not give an aldehyde upon the addition of water.

1-Butyne is a linear alkyne with the chemical formula C4H6. When 1-butyne reacts with water, it undergoes an addition reaction to form a ketone, not an aldehyde. Therefore, 1-butyne does not give an aldehyde upon the addition of water.
When 2-butyne reacts with water, it undergoes addition reaction to form a ketone, not an aldehyde.Therefore, 2-butyne does not give an aldehyde upon the addition of water.

questions.explanation: Ethyne, also known as acetylene, is an alkyne with the chemical formula C2H2. When ethyne reacts with water in the presence of a mercuric sulfate catalyst, it undergoes an addition reaction to form acetaldehyde. Acetaldehyde is an aldehyde. Therefore, ethyne (acetylene) does give an aldehyde upon the addition of water.

questions.text: Which of the following is not acidic in nature
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Only terminal alkynes show the acidic character because sp hybridized carbon atoms of a terminal alkyne pulls the elelctrons more strongly making the attached hydrogen atom slightly acidic. 2-Butyne is not acidic because it is not terminal alkyne.

questions.text: Total number of hybrid orbitals of all the carbon atoms in benzene are:
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Benzene consists of six sp2-hybridized carbon atoms each sp2-carbon atoms has 3sp2 hybrid orbital therefore the total number of hybrid orbitals in benzene would be 18.

questions.text: The resonance energy of benzene is
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Resonance energy of benzene is +150.5 kJmol-1

questions.text: The conversion of benzene into cyclohexane is_
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: The conversion of benzene to cyclohexane is hydrogenation. This process of hydrogenation is an exothermic as it gives out 208kJ/mol of energy.

questions.text: The introduction of an alkyl group in benzene takes place in the presence of AlCl3 and the reagent is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Refer to the main text for explanation.
Refer to the main text for explanation.
Refer to the main text for explanation.
Refer to the main text for explanation.

questions.explanation: The introduction of an alkyl group (an alkyl substitution) into benzene takes place through a Friedel-Crafts alkylation reaction. The reagent used in this reaction is the alkyl halide (alkyl halogenide) or alkyl chloride (alkyl chlorides are a specific type of alkyl halide). The reaction is catalyzed by a Lewis acid, typically aluminum chloride (AlCl3) or ferric chloride (FeCl3). The general reaction scheme for Friedel-Crafts alkylation is as follows: Benzene + R-X (alkyl halide) → Alkylbenzene + HX (hydrogen halide).Where R is the alkyl group (e.g., methyl, ethyl, propyl, etc.), X is the halogen (e.g., Cl, Br, I), and HX is the hydrogen halide formed as a byproduct. Therefore, option B is correct.

questions.text: o- and p-nitrophenol are formed by the reaction of _ with phenol at 25°C:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Incorrect option.The reaction of nitric acid with phenol is an electrophilic substitution reaction. In this reaction, the nitric acid molecule donates an electrophile to the phenol molecule. The electrophile attacks the phenol molecule at the ortho and para positions, forming o- and p-nitrophenol.

The reaction of nitric acid with phenol is an electrophilic substitution reaction. In this reaction, the nitric acid molecule donates an electrophile to the phenol molecule. The electrophile attacks the phenol molecule at the ortho and para positions, forming o- and p-nitrophenol. The mechanism is shown below.
